title: tetraberlinia microphylla
intro: Tetraberlinia microphylla, also known as the 'Red Ironwood', is a slow-growing tree species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive reddish-brown heartwood. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Tetraberlinia microphylla include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Tetraberlinia microphylla are often made by decocting the bark, leaves, or roots in water, and are traditionally used in various parts of Africa to treat a range of ailments.
